title: astragalus unguiculatus
intro: Astragalus unguiculatus, also known as milk vetch or Huang qi, is a type of flowering plant native to Mongolia and China. It has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for centuries, prized for its adaptogenic and immune-boosting properties. The plant's medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial effects, which contribute to its health benefits, such as reducing stress and fatigue, improving immune function, and enhancing physical performance. Astragalus unguiculatus is often prepared as a decoction, capsule, or tincture, and is also used in combination with other herbs in traditional medicine.
